sourceNotes: Column causes the magic of its glyph to manifest in a column or beam above the glyph. If the signs are not balanced, the spell manifests from the side with the most column signs toward the opposite side of the ring. A column sign on the left pushes the effect to the right. The shorter line on the sign typically faces outwards, and while it is sometimes shown inverted, it is unclear how this changes the effect.
Dispersion causes a seal's magic to pour or disperse outward. The wiki notes that both normal and inverted forms have appeared, and that inverted column may overlap with this behavior.

Levitation causes the spell's magic to float above the glyph, or causes the movement of the object it is drawn on. If the latter, the movement depends on which direction the signs point. The pointed, arrow-like side of the sign typically faces inwards.
Pull draws material matching the seal's sigil toward the seal when the arrow portion points inward. It is likely to push outward when inverted, and angled signs may add twisting motion.
Crush breaks material apart. With earth it can reduce objects to powder, while inverted crush can reform powdered material into its prior shape while the spell lasts.
Float is a non-directional sign that makes things float in midair. Most examples levitate the object bearing the seal, though some uses appear to need targeting support from other signs.
Region determines where magic manifests relative to a seal: to one side, within the ring, outside the ring, or directly on the ring depending on sign arrangement.

Convergence causes the magic of its spell to become more focused, centered down to a single point. It can also make loose particles become more rigid, as explained by the disciples of Qifrey's Atelier when they were designing the serpent's bed of sand, saying that it would cause the sand the spell produced to become hard and compact. One of the triangle's points is typically seen pointing inwards.
Collection gathers material above or around the seal so the spell can use it. Its open side is usually shown facing inward.
Billow lets a spell produce a fluffy cloud-like result and may need collection-like support to gather material. Its exact behavior remains uncertain.
Repetition resets affected objects to a previous state. The wiki notes it was historically treated as a sign but was later retconned into a sigil.
Weave, also called ribbon, turns affected solid objects into long flexible ribbons on contact with the seal and is meant to surround the central sigil.
Cool presumably cools material down. It appears in the vapor bubble spell, likely for chilling or condensation behavior.
Strengthen presumably makes objects stronger or more durable. The wiki lists it as a semi-directional sign introduced in volume 12 bonus material.
Sights set appears to help aim a spell at a point or target and may support mind-directed control, especially in capture pennant-style spells.
Entwine makes an object, such as a ribbon, wrap or entwine around other objects. The exact mechanism is unknown.
Sign of wind is an asymmetric sign connected to wind magic. Its function beyond that relation is unclear after later material reaffirmed its canon status.
Aeriforms defined resembles wind-sigil modifiers and can modify wind underfoot in the pegasus carriage spell, though its exact function is unclear.
Gather resembles collection by letting a spell use surrounding material. The wiki suggests it may actively draw material in rather than passively collect it.
Glaives determine how deeply magic embeds itself into animal flesh. The wiki notes they may not be ordinary signs, but they are listed among sign-like forms.
Diamond most likely causes a spell to affect nearby objects while not affecting the object bearing the seal.
Window most likely causes a spell to affect only the object it is drawn on, contrasting with diamond in enlarge/reduction spell comparisons.
Enlarge causes objects to grow, or shrink when inverted. Window and diamond signs seem to control whether the target is the drawn object or nearby objects.
Crosshair likely causes magic to target or manifest in objects with a corresponding magical aspect. It appears in rainflinger and fish guiding seals.
Radial's exact function is unclear, but it likely decreases or softens a spell's power, such as converting fire into heat in snugstone-like usage.
Bolt makes magic manifest as bolt-like projectiles. With region, those projectiles can be fired in a chosen direction with dangerous speed.
Eye appears with illusion and light-manipulation spells. Its isolated effect is unknown, and the wiki notes it may actually be a sigil.
Vision is related to sight, visual perception, or illusion. It can support illusions with eye or aid sight in light-reducing spell work.
Bend appears in spells involving shadow, petrification, and wall bending. Its common theme is altering or bending perception, matter, or reality.
Rain makes a spell produce a rainfall-like effect in its immediate area. It is meant to surround the central sigil and can be inverted inside-out.
Puppet appears to allow a user to mentally control movement of the object bearing the spell, with movement depending on the paired sigil.
Bind likely attaches the spell securely to a touched surface or holds the affected object together. Its exact function is unknown.
Orb creates a spherical space above the seal where controlled material can collect, filling from the bottom upward when material is added.
Purify separates impurities from the magic of its sigil, such as contaminants from water or dust from air, leaving the separated material near the spell.
Link connects magic between the object bearing the sign and other objects broken from, removed from, or made out of the same original object.
